Psychiatry & Neuromodulation

TMS for Anxiety

FDA-cleared TMS therapy for anxious depression, providing a drug-free pathway to calm the overactive mind without sedation or cognitive fog.

Schedule a Consultation Explore Therapy
FDA
Cleared Therapy
Non-Med
Sedation-Free
Targeted
Neuromodulation
Outpt.
20-Min Sessions
Understanding Treatment

Calming the Circuitry of Worry

How specialized TMS protocols can down-regulate the neuro-networks responsible for generalized and persistent anxiety.

The Anxious Brain How TMS Calms Symptoms Addressed
Section 01

The Neurological Basis of Anxiety

Anxiety is a state of neurological hyperarousal, not a character flaw.

Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD) is characterized by excessive, uncontrollable worry that interferes with daily life. Brain imaging shows that patients with chronic anxiety often exhibit hyperactivity in the right Dorsolateral Prefrontal Cortex (DLPFC) and an over-reactive amygdala, the brain's "fear center."

When these regions are stuck in overdrive, the nervous system remains in a constant state of "fight or flight," preventing relaxation, destroying sleep, and causing physical symptoms like muscle tension and rapid heart rate.

While depression treatments often focus on "waking up" the left side of the brain, anxiety treatments focus on "calming down" the right side.

Section 02

Applying the Brakes

Using inhibitory frequencies to soothe an overactive nervous system.

Using FDA-cleared protocols for anxious depression, TMS leverages low-frequency magnetic pulses—typically applied to the right side of the brain. Rather than stimulating, these specific frequencies are inhibitory.

These pulses work to down-regulate the hyperactive neural networks. Over a series of sessions, the brain learns to maintain this calmer state, allowing the amygdala to stop misfiring and restoring a sense of baseline calm.

01

Inhibitory Pulses

Low-frequency magnetic waves act as a "brake" on hyper-excitable cortical neurons.

02

Network Soothing

Dampening activity in the prefrontal cortex cascades down to soothe the deep-brain fear centers.

03

Dual Treatment

For patients with comorbid depression, Dr. Amin can apply a dual-target protocol during the same session.

Section 03

Symptoms Managed by TMS

Reducing both the mental and physical manifestations of chronic anxiety.

Racing Thoughts

Decreasing the severity of rumination, catastrophic thinking, and persistent, uncontrollable worry.

Physical Tension

Alleviating the chronic muscle tightness, jaw clenching, and bodily restlessness associated with GAD.

Sleep Disturbances

Helping patients initiate and maintain sleep by down-regulating nighttime cognitive hyperarousal.

The Benefits of Non-Systemic Care

Why Choose TMS for Anxiety?

A powerful alternative to benzodiazepines and sedating antidepressants.

No Sedation or Fog

Unlike common anti-anxiety medications (like Xanax or Ativan), TMS does not sedate you, slow your reaction times, or cause cognitive dulling.

Non-Addictive

TMS carries zero risk of chemical dependency, tolerance building, or withdrawal symptoms, making it an incredibly safe long-term solution.

Dual Efficacy

Because anxiety and depression so frequently co-occur, Dr. Amin can apply a bilateral TMS protocol to address both conditions concurrently in the same visit.

Expertise in Interventional Psychiatry

Meet Dr. Ritesh Amin, MD

Board Certified Psychiatrist

Dr. Amin is highly experienced in the nuanced application of neuromodulation. Recognizing that many patients with depression also suffer from debilitating anxiety, he customizes TMS mapping. By expertly targeting the neural circuitry responsible for hyperarousal, he provides comprehensive relief that traditional monotherapies often miss.

Read Full Biography
Frequently Asked Questions

TMS for Anxiety FAQs

TMS is FDA-cleared as a treatment for Anxious Depression, meaning it is officially recognized for treating anxiety symptoms that co-occur with Major Depressive Disorder. While off-label for pure GAD (without depression), the clinical protocols are widely used by experts with highly successful outcomes.
Some patients feel nervous before their first session simply due to the unknown. However, the treatment itself is comfortable. You sit in a reclining chair, and the pulses feel like a tapping sensation on your scalp. In fact, many patients find the sessions relaxing and use the time to meditate or listen to soothing music.
Patient Stories

Real People, Real Results

Hear from patients whose lives were transformed through Dr. Amin's care.

M

Marietta Baranca

★★★★★
" I've been dealing with depression and PTSD for years and Dr. Amin's TMS Therapy has honestly saved my life. I'm someone who does not respond well to depression medication, so TMS was a godsend for me. I went from crying for no reason almost every day to feeling more stable and even optimistic. Dr. Amin is really invested in my mental health and Charlie, the TMS technician, is very helpful in making the treatments go by quickly and swiftly.
Google Review
S

Syeda Shebah Ali

★★★★★
" Before becoming a patient of Dr. Amin, I was experiencing persistent cognitive fog, chronic depression, and generalized anxiety that significantly impacted my ability to function day to day. Since implementing Dr. Amin's comprehensive recommendations, my cognitive clarity has returned, my mood has stabilized, and I now have the energy and confidence to manage daily tasks. Dr. Amin's thoughtful and integrative approach has been transformative — his attention to detail and commitment to addressing root causes rather than just symptoms has helped me reclaim my quality of life.
Google Review
